Hello friends and welcome back to Automated Intimacy. In this lesson, we're going to revisit a concept I call coaching the conversion. It's actually a process, a paradigm, a way of thinking about marketing that I developed back in around 2017-2018. It's been the main concept behind two really successful courses of mine. Ten X launches and Ten X sales pages were both built around this idea of coaching the conversion. Both of them have enrolled thousands of students inside of copy school by copy hackers. It's also been taught inside Amy Porterfield's Digital Course Academy, inside of Danny Eney's Miraces, Selma course's membership and has been in guest training and guest webinars for platforms like Thinkific, Teachable, and a few others I believe that I'm just not recalling right now. It's been taught widely, it's been shared widely, and it's really something I developed in response to what I was noticing in the marketplace. That was that markets were getting more sophisticated. Prospects, consumers' leads were becoming more skeptical and no one wanted to be bludgeoned into a sale or have their arms twisted until they finally gave in and caved. Instead, prospects were craving transformation and it wasn't just what they were craving, it's what they were responding to. Responsiveness, direct response wasn't a matter of blunt force persuasion but actually empowering someone with the ability to make an informed choice and an informed decision. It's all built around coaching a transformation in your copy, in your marketing, and TenX Launch has focused heavily on the email side of things, TenX sales pages, obviously on the sales page side of things. But yeah, things have changed and technology has gotten better. Both the emails and TenX launches and the sales page and TenX sales pages are still those more static assets that are part of that more linear Newtonian funnel that we discussed in the last lesson. Where this is evolving right now is integrating the best of that coaching, the conversion ethos, and instead bringing in more of those intimate touch points, those quantum moments that we couldn't really execute to full effect before without the automation side, without having a system, a CRM that could actually keep track of where people were at, what they were interacting with, that minority report marketing that gathers those signals from your prospects actions and really sees who is ready for that more intimate touch point so that we don't burn ourselves out literally messaging everybody on our email list directly. So the technology is here and it is time to evolve coaching the conversion to 2.0. But that said, it's still built upon just two core principles. And the first one is that a sale or a conversion is nothing but coaching the transformation between point A and point buyer. Or as I like to say, a pre customer who is your prospect to a customer, which is the sale all the way to an ascended customer and that would be the lifetime value because yes, you are still selling them on taking action based on the course or coaching program they invested in. And that helps set up the future sale and the lifetime value of it. So everything is transformation. Everything is about evolving someone from one state to the next to the next and marketing is just what we do to empower and engage our prospects in that transformation. Cool. So this is what it looks like. Old paradigm on a funnel we had marketing, we had sales. And then finally, if they bought our program, we would have the coaching and the marketing is where we have our pre customers and the sales is where we turn them into a customer and the coaching is where hopefully we give enough value and give enough results that they become a lifetime customer and we extract the lifetime value. And typically transformation and intimacy would go up. They would increase as we moved up that pyramid. There'd be less intimacy and less transformation in the marketing at the base to pre customers or to leads or to prospects, however you want to frame it. There'd be a little bit more on the sales side when we're actually getting involved in a call or a direct messaging conversation. And then finally coaching those are our clients, obviously that's where we would have the most transformation and intimacy. And that is still true intimacy and transformation do go up as people ascend those levels of the funnel or the automated conversion ecosystems. But the main difference is in the coaching the conversion method. We view everything as coaching. We don't view it as marketing sales and then finally coaching. Marketing is coaching sales is coaching. And of course, well, coaching is still coaching. So the ACEs the automated conversion ecosystems are what we're going to be using to coach our pre customers into customers and into that lifetime value. Now in this program, we're going to be focusing primarily on the marketing and the sales aspects of the pyramid. So that's going to be ACE one, ACE two and ACE three in future sessions and future lessons, we will look more at what is actually happening once you have a customer, what are the signals we're looking for there in order to serve them fully and encourage them to evolve into lifetime customers or ascended customers. So that's a conversation for another day. But for now, it's enough to know that we are treating every stage of your funnel, every conversion ecosystem as an opportunity to coach our prospects into the next rung, the next part of the transformation, the next part of the intimacy of our engagement with them. Cool. And the point is that as a world class coach or educator or trainer or leader, why would you wait until cash changes hands before literally leveraging your greatest gift? So the important reframe here that I want you to lead with is you don't have leads in a funnel, you have pre customers in need of a certain transformation, a transformation in identity, in belief, and state and in action. And here's something to keep in mind, leads versus pre customers. If we fully acknowledged that every single person who enters your sphere of influence or ace has the ability to become a customer, that there's a latent potential and it's up to us as the conversion coach to unlock it, understand what they need and facilitate and empower that transformation. So this reminds me of something called the observer effect. If we're seeing leads as pre customers, they feel a little bit closer to being customers, right? And now we're doing our work to remove the pre of the observer effect, which is from the world of quantum physics. In our context, when you treat leads like high value customers, as if they've already become one, something magical happens, they start feeling valued and they actually start reorienting around how we view view and how you experience them. And maybe you've had this experience in your own life, where you're embodying or carrying a certain state. And somebody see something in you that you don't really see yourself as yet, and they start treating you that way. And you all of a sudden start shifting to start reorienting around how they are relating to you, because we don't like being in this mismatch where someone's relating to us as something we're not. So if someone says you are just full of energy and you're amazing and I love the energy you bring to the room, all of a sudden you might find yourself starting to actually live into the identity that they've ascribed to you in that moment. So the observer effect works like that in our marketing. If we see our pre customers, our leads, as someone who will eventually become a customer, if we actually start treating them like a customer right now, and valuing them as somebody who has already invested in us, we in turn will start investing in them, giving them more of our energy, more of our presence, feeling great about that exchange, and they'll start reorienting around how we view them. Cool. So the question to leave you with here is how would you show up differently? If you believed that everyone you're engaging with right now as a prospect will inevitably become a customer, and it's an important question to dwell in, but it's also an important question to put into action even just for one day, three days, one week, every new prospect you engage with, how would you show up if you believed that they would be a high value customer? And how can you start engaging with them as if they already are one? Cool. So doing this is the bridge between our earning potential and our earned reality, what we can be generating and what we actually will generate. If we could turn every pre customer, everyone who should become a customer into a customer, then all of a sudden we've bridged that gap and we really are operating at our fullest potential. So this is really it. This is where growth happened. It's in that transformation between point a point buyer, which is also to say pre customer to customer. And that is the gap between our earning potential and our earned reality, where everyone who stands to benefit from what we do has a chance to actually feel empowered to take us up on that invitation. So that is the true north we are going going for here. We are going for the true north of having everyone in our ecosystem who can benefit from our services and is empowered to actually go through with it to have the invitation at the right time and the right leadership and guidance to actually become a buyer. Cool. So when you embrace all of this, it's no longer about selling. It's not not about selling. It's not something we have an aversion or a resistant or any weird feelings around. But we actually look at something greater and we realize that it's about your pre customer experiencing a transformation within your marketing and sales process, not just of belief, but identity, self identity and identity with a new reality that makes your offer a natural and frictionless pathway. So to recap, your marketing has to coach the conversion between point a and point buyer, which is really to say from pre customer to customer. So the goal is to remove the pre and you don't force it off. It sheds itself like a snake, sheds its skin or like the cocoon dissolves. So the pre you could think of as a protective layer. It's a barrier from a change that they're resisting and a natural skepticism that they may still have in coaches, courses or sales. Cool. So the next principle is that of micro conversions and precursors. So rather than focusing on conversion as a huge, single major event, instead we identify the micro conversions that preceded it and coach our pre customers through those smaller, more digestible milestones. And we do this because forcing too much change too fast in belief, identity and action causes that opposite reaction of fear, friction and resistance. So the way I like to think about this is you don't knock over a 20 ton domino with blunt force. That is persuasion. That is a willfulness. Instead, you gently tap the first one and let that momentum carry all the way till the end. And that to me is the difference between persuasion and leadership based coaching. So a conversion should ultimately slide effortlessly into place. And most marketing and sales even today seem to try to jam it all together. Try to fit these pieces that just won't fit, which is why most customers or prospects feel battered, bruised and even betrayed by the process. So in the next lesson, we'll go over what I call the anatomy of an empowered high value customer and the five core conversion precursors that we'll be coaching across the entire program. So when you're ready, take a breather and I'll meet you there in just a few moments.
